2606.31903.title: Attend, Transform, or Silence: Operator-Level Visual Skipping for Efficient Multimodal LLM Inference
2606.31903.paper_summary: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) increasingly process long visual-token sequences, increasing the overall inference computation. Existing acceleration methods usually remove visual tokens or skip visual-token updates in entire layers, but these coarse strategies may discard fine-grained evidence or suppress useful operators together with redundant ones. In this paper, we study visual-token computation from an answer-observable perspective and find that late visual-token updates can remain large while having little effect on answer-token representations. Motivated by this answer-silent redundancy, we decompose each Transformer layer into attention and FFN operators and show that useful visual computation is often operator-dominant and layer-dependent. We propose an operator-level visual-token skipping framework that preserves the full visual-token sequence while selectively bypassing redundant attention, FFN, or both. Experiments across three MLLM architectures and 10 VQA benchmarks show that our method achieves strong efficiency-accuracy trade-offs, reducing \textbf{33.7\%} TFLOPs on Qwen3-VL while retaining \textbf{99.5\%} of the vanilla model performance.
2606.31903.paper_summary_zh: 多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）越來越多地處理長的視覺標記序列，這增加了整體推理計算。現有的加速方法通常會刪除視覺標記或跳過整個層的視覺標記更新，但這些粗略的策略可能會丟棄細粒度的證據或將有用的運算符與冗餘的運算符一起抑制。在本文中，我們從答案可觀察的角度研究視覺標記計算，並發現晚期的視覺標記更新可以保持較大，同時對答案標記表示的影響很小。受到這種答案靜默冗餘的啟發，我們將每個Transformer層分解為注意力和FFN運算符，並顯示有用的視覺計算通常是運算符主導且層依賴的。我們提出了一個運算符級別的視覺標記跳過框架，該框架保留完整的視覺標記序列，同時有選擇地繞過冗餘的注意力、FFN或兩者。跨越三個MLLM架構和10個VQA基準的實驗顯示，我們的方法實現了強大的效率-準確性權衡，在保持\textbf{99.5\%}的原始模型性能的同時，將Qwen3-VL上的TFLOPs減少了\textbf{33.7\%}。
2606.31903.author: Zhaoyang Luo et.al.
2606.31903.authors: Zhaoyang Luo, Runmin Dong, Miao Yang, Fan Wei, Yushan Lai, Bin Luo, Haohuan Fu
